lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Please let me in Dorgesh-city!

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: I'm sorry, but surface goblins are not allowed into the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Grubfoot? What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: [player name]! I so glad you here! You help me get into Dorgesh-city? I want talk to Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Chosen Commander? You mean Zanik?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Yes, that her. I have dream about her, remember, before you come to Goblin Village. She Chosen Commander who lead all goblins to new age.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Generals still not sure, but Grubfoot sure. She Chosen Commander!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mistag, why won't you let Grubfoot into the city?

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Our laws don't currently allow surface goblins to enter. The council wants to extend a hand of friendship to their kind, but we have heard that they are a very warlike people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take responsibility for Grubfoot. I know he won't cause any trouble.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Hmm... If you are prepared to vouch for him, then I suppose we can trust him.

lines.speaker: Mistag
lines.text: Very well, Grubfoot. You may enter Dorgesh-Kaan, but you must stay with [player name] until you leave.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]! I promise I be no trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll let you two sort this out yourselves.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Hello, [player name]! Will you take me into Dorgesh-city now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow me.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I follow you, but you should pick up pet thingy first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have time right now.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Will you take me into Dorgesh-city now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Okay. I go back to wait in Dorgesh-mines.

sections.title: If the player walks too far

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I want go into Dorgesh-city, not go that way. I meet you back at Dorgesh mines.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: So big...so many Dorgesh-goblins...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Chosen Commander...

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: You talk to her for me, [player name]! I too nervous!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: What are you doing in the city, surface goblin?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I looking for Chosen Commander!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Commander of the guard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He means Zanik.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Zanik's not any kind of commander. She'd never fit into a command structure, she's too independant.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Anyway, I think I saw her with Oldak earlier. You should check his lab.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: A surface goblin in the city? That's against the rules!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mistag said that Grubfoot could come in as long as he stayed with me.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hmm... Carry on, then. But I'm keeping an eye on you.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm keeping an eye on you, surface gobiln.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I not make any trouble!

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: What happened to your eyes?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Nothing wrong with my eyes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Grubfoot is a goblin from the surface. They all have eyes that size.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Oh! Welcome to the city, surface goblin! What are you doing down here?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I looking for Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He means Zanik.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Zanik? She's no commander... I think she's in Oldak's lab at the moment.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Eek! A goblin!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... you're a goblin!

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Yes, but a goblin from the surface...

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I not mean any harm. Just here to see Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Chosen Commander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He means Zanik.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Zanik? I should hope that fool isn't in command of anything!

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Where is Chosen Commander?

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He means Zanik.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Yes. Zanik is Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: Oh? Chosen by whom?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Big High War God! He choose Zanik to lead all goblins into new age!

lines.speaker: Cave goblin
lines.text: The war god? Oh no!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay! Zanik isn't in league with any war god.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Grubfoot, you probably shouldn't mention the Big High War God down here. He's not exactly popular with the Dorgeshuun.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: A surface goblin? What are you doing in the city?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Miner person say I can come in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mistag said that Grubfoot could come into the city as long as he stayed with me.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: That is somewhat irregular, but I suppose it is reasonable. Welcome to the city, surface goblin! What brings you to Dorgesh-Kaan?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I looking for Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He means Zanik.

lines.speaker: Ur-tag
lines.text: Ah yes, of course. I think she has been helping Oldak with his experiments recently. You should check Oldak's lab.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hello, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Zanik! There's someone here to see you.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Chosen Commander?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Remarkable! You must be a goblin from the surface, correct?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Yes. I am goblin.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I recognize you. You were in Goblin Village, weren't you? You said you had a dream about me.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Yes. You Chosen Commander. Other goblins not believe, but I believe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't other goblins believe?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: They say Zanik not true goblin. She cannot be Chosen Commander because she not worship Big High War God. Because she not think war good.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I certainly don't!

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: But does that mean she have no destiny? Perhaps her destiny to lead goblins into new age of peace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's an unusual sentiment for a goblin.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Yes. I thought all surface goblins thought war was good.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Most do, but not me. I see goblins kill goblins, humans kill goblins. I think, is this really best thing in life?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I not say that to other goblins. They kill me if they think I not like war.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the Chosen Commander?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Goblin legend. Hopespear, long ago goblin, he have dream. He say someday Chosen Commander come, lead all goblins to new age.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Most goblins, they think Chosen Commander lead goblins to war and fight other races. But I think maybe not.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I have dreams that Zanik is Chosen Commander. Before you visit Goblin Village I dream about Zanik. And now, last night I have new dream.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why have you come to talk to Zanik?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: To tell Chosen Commander about new dream I have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was this new dream?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Yes, what was it?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I have dream of far-off land. It like no land I ever see, but somehow I know it.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: It cool and wet. Ground is soft, big plants everywhere, red water bubbling.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I see goblins, many goblins. Goblins of all tribes, but they not fight. They have no weapons. They live in peace.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Then I see Chosen Commander. She stand at entrance to new land, she show way in. She lead all goblins on long journey to new land.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I never see land before. I see it only in dream. But somehow I know what it is. It Yu'biusk, Land of the Goblins.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik/Oldak after the cutscene

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... What's Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: That goblin legend. Land of Goblins. Legend say long ago, even before war of gods, goblins come from Yu'biusk. Big High War God find them in Yu'biusk and take them to other lands.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Most goblins forget legend. Think only of war. But I think, if Zanik lead us back to Yu'biusk, maybe we have peace. Maybe we be safe from people who want to kill us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know your dream was true?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Yes, how do you know? Dorgeshuun scientists and philosophers generally believe that dreams are a way for the mind to sort through memories. There is no known mechanism by which they can predict the future.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: This dream not like other dream. It so real.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What do you think, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just a dream. It doesn't mean anything.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: It not just dream! It true! I know in heart!

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: I'm sorry, Grubfoot, but wanting something badly doesn't make it true.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Even so... if there's any chance there might be something in it, we have to investigate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it must mean something.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]! I know you believe me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: It's probably nothing, but if there's even a small chance there's something in it, we have to investigate.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: So where is this promised land, Grubfoot?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I not know. Not know any goblin who know. But...

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: But what?

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I should not tell! Not in front of non-goblin!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name] is a friend. You can tell [him/her/them].

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I suppose...

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: There secret place. Secret temple to Big High War God. No one know of it but goblins. Priests in secret temple, I bet they know way to Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: But before I tell you where temple is, you must make promise. Promise not to tell any other non-goblins where temple is. If humans find out, maybe they attack, kill all goblins in temple.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I promise, Grubfoot. I won't tell anyone where the temple is.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: If that is what is required to keep your temple safe, then I promise too.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: [player name], you must promise as well. That very important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I promise not to tell anyone where the temple is.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I trust you, [player name]. You save village from H.A.M. attack. Now I tell you where temple is.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: It built long ago, on Plain of Mud. It sink into mud and now it underground in muddy caves. It far west of here, near place where many humans catch fish.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hmm... I've heard of a cave system to the west where many goblins live. That must be where this temple is hidden. I can make teleport spheres to take you there.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: I can't go. I must get back to village before generals miss me.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Oh, that's a pity. Just a minute...

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Here, break this sphere on the ground. It will take you back to the Goblin Village.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Thank you. I go now, but you must go to temple and find way to Yu'biusk.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik/Oldak after Grubfoot leaves

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: [player name], Zanik, are you ready to leave?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm ready!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Then let's go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not ready.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'll wait until you are, [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Oldak again before entering the Goblin Temple

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. What did you find in the Goblin Temple?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We haven't gone in yet.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Ah, well you'd better get back there then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you later.

sections.title: Talking to Grubfoot before going to Yu'Biusk

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Did you go to Land of Goblins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Shut up Grubfoot!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Shut up Grubfoot!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The entrance to the secret temple must be around here somewhere, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Okay, [player name]. I'll meet you back at the cave entrance.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: Go away, human! We already tell you not to come in!

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: Only goblins allowed in. If you goblin, you come in. But you not goblin, you stupid stinky puny human.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: Who you?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: Dorgesh tribe?

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: Me hear Dorgesh tribe return from below ground.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You want get into temple, Dorgesh-goblin?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Yes please!

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: You goblin, you come in.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: Human! Where you think you going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you said we could go in!

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: No, we say Dorgesh-goblin go in. You human, you go away!

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: Humans not allowed! Only goblins allowed!

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. Did you speak to the Makeover Mage?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: You really should. I believe they live south west of the human city of Falador.

sections.title: Attempting to pick Pharmakos berries before talking to the Makeover Mage

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. What did you find in the Goblin Temple?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Humans aren't allowed inside. They let Zanik in, but not me.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hmm... that is an interesting problem. But I think there is a way to get you in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I get into the temple without being a goblin?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Perhaps you can't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? But you said you had a way.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Yes, I think you can get into the temple, but not without being a goblin. I have been in correspondence with some of the wizards who live on the surface. I know of one who might be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: They are known as the Makeover Mage. You should go and talk to them. I believe they live south west of the human city of Falador.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you teleport me to the temple again?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: I can, but I can't afford to keep doing it for free. If you get me two law runes and a piece of molten glass, I'll make a sphere to take you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have to go get some law runes and molten glass then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I'll walk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and see this mage.

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: Hello there! I am known as the Makeover Mage! I have spent many years researching magics that can change your physical appearance!

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: I can alter your physical form with my magic, there's no fee. Would you like me to perform my magics upon you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you turn me into a goblin?

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: Into a goblin? What do you want to turn into a goblin for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to slip past some goblin guards.

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: Really? What are they guarding?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I promised not to tell anyone.

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: Okay, okay, sorry I asked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you turn me into a goblin or not?

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: I think I know how, but I've never deliberately transmogrified someone into another species before. It's a very risky procedure, and I don't want to be held responsible if it goes wrong.

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: How about I tell you the recipe so you can make the potion yourself. It's not too complex. You'll just need to take a toadflax potion and add some pharmakos berries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pharmakos berries?

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: That's right. They're quite rare, but have wonderful properties that help with transmogrification. Luckily, I grow plenty right outside. Feel free to take some.

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: Now, once you've made the potion, remember that the transformation is very delicate. It will reverse itself if you are in direct sunlight or if you perform a strenuous activity like fighting.

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: For that reason, you should only drink it in the area where you're going to need it. I'd also suggest you take off any pieces of armour or similar before drinking it, just in case the transformation damages them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just like goblins.

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: What a strange thing to want to turn into.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't want to be a goblin.

sections.title: Talking to the Makeover Mage again

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: Hello there! I am known as the Makeover Mage! I have spent many years researching magics that can change your physical appearance!

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: I can alter your physical form with my magic, there's no fee. Would you like me to perform my magics upon you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you turn me into a goblin?

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: You can do it yourself using that potion I told you about. Just take a toadflax potion and add some pharmakos berries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pharmakos berries?

lines.speaker: Makeover Mage
lines.text: That's right. They're quite rare, but have wonderful properties that help with transmogrification. Luckily, I grow plenty right outside. Feel free to take some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Thanks for the help.

sections.title: Talking to Oldak after talking to the Makeover Mage

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. Any luck getting into the Goblin Temple?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but I spoke to the Makeover Mage. They told me how to make a potion to turn myself into a goblin.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Excellent! You'd better head back to the temple and try this potion out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: What you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to get into the temple please.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: Why you talk strange?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... Err...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me want get into temple?

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: That better.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: What your name, goblin?

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: No wait, I guess.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: Is it [goblin name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, me [goblin name].

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: Ha ha! Me knew me guess right!

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You not come into temple with that thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What thing?

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: That thing you carrying! It look alive!

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: Goblin guard: That thing! That thing that following you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My follower?

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: Me not like it.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You go take it somewhere else before you go in.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You go into temple now, [goblin name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. You guess again.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (1)
lines.text: Is it [goblin name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, me no want to go in temple.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You go away then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me want get into temple.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: What your name, goblin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You go away then.

sections.title: Talking to the Goblin guards again

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You want go into temple, [goblin name]?

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: What is it then?

lines.speaker: Goblin guard (2)
lines.text: You go away then.

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: You goblins listen!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: This is what Big High War God say:

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Slay enemies of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Enemies must die!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Not to run from battle!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Cowards must die!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Not to show mercy!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Merciful must die!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Not to doubt Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Doubters must die!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Not to make own plans!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Thinkers must die!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: In beginning, goblins weak.

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Big High War God make us strong!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: In beginning, goblins have no commander.

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Big High War God send commander!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: In time of great war...

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: He send commander to lead us.

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Now we have no commander.

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: But some day he send new commander!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: New commander leads goblins to victory!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Goblins conquer all of world!

lines.speaker: Preacher
lines.text: Then Big High War God return!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Me hear Dorgesh tribe come back from underground. Maybe now all tribes united, prophecy will come true! Chosen Commander come and lead all tribes to victory over world!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Me hear there ghost in temple crypt! No one see it, but sometimes they hear it. 'Wooo... wooo...' it say.

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Me mighty warrior of Big High War God! Me find farmer working in field, and me attack! He so scared he run off! Then me trample on crops! This deeds of great warrior of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Me glad me finally get to secret goblin temple! Me walk long way to get here, always in danger of humans. But now me here, me safe! There no humans here!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Me hate humans! When me leave temple, me go and kill many human children!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Shh! Not to talk in temple!

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Praise Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me have question.

lines.speaker: Goblin
lines.text: Questions for High Priest. He the one that knows stuff.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You go away! Only [tribe name] tribe come in here!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You can pass!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Praise Big High War God!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: May the Big High War God fill your soul with the glory of war!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: May the Big High War God fill your eyes with hate for his enemies!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: May the Big High War God clear your mind of all thoughts!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: May the Big High War God fill your heart with courage to die for him!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: May the Big High War God fill your arms with strength to do his bidding!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where Zanik?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Zanik? Who is Zanik?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She Dorgeshuun goblin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She Chosen Commander.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Chosen Commander? You have history mixed up. Chosen Commander come in future, not now. When Chosen Commander come, I know, and I not see any Chosen Commander today.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... well she Dorgeshuun goblin.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Yes, there was goblin who say she Dorgesh-goblin. She have big eyes and she speak in strange accent. She ask about Yu'biusk, but she not pass test.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: She not just not know answers. Lots of goblins not know answers. She start shouting crazy ideas about how war is bad and we not need gods.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Huzamogaarb guards, they grab her and they put her in prison in Huzamogaarb enclave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How me get into Huzamogaarb enclave?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Only Huzamogaarb tribe allowed in the enclave. That not you. You wearing wrong armour. The Huzamogaarb tribe wear black armour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How they get black armour?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: They use dye to make armour black. Other tribes do similar, but in own tribe colour instead.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Yellow for Yurkolgokh, white for Saragorgak, purple for Horogothgar, orange for Narogoshuun, blue for Ekeleshuun and like said, black for Huzamogaarb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where they get dye?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Some goblins say they go to witch-lady in Draynor for it. Me hope they beat up witch-lady and take dye! That way of Big High War God!

sections.title: Talking to High Priest Bighead again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where Zanik?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You forgetful? I told you, guards put her in prison in Huzamogaarb enclave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How me get into Huzamogaarb enclave?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Only Huzamogaarb tribe allowed in the enclave. That not you. You wearing wrong armour. The Huzamogaarb tribe wear black armour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How they get black armour?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: They use dye to make armour black. Other tribes do similar, but in own tribe colour instead.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Yellow for Yurkolgokh, white for Saragorgak, purple for Horogothgar, orange for Narogoshuun, blue for Ekeleshuun and like said, black for Huzamogaarb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where they get dye?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Some goblins say they go to witch-lady in Draynor for it. Me hope they beat up witch-lady and take dye! That way of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. Have you discovered anything in the Goblin Temple?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The goblins there have imprisoned Zanik!

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Oh no! You'd better find a way to get her out!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What do you want now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik! It's me, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I turned myself into a goblin so I could get in here!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You did what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes... Definitely one of my more interesting choices.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I'll say. You can tell me all about it later. For now, I could do with a bit of a hand getting out of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I managed to upset the high priest. He said he'd tell me about Yu'biusk if I passed a test on goblin religion. I don't think he liked my answers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Clearly. Well luckily I have a moving-over-distance sphere here. You can use it to teleport out.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]! I'm sorry to leave you, but it looks like you'll have to find out about Yu'biusk on your own. Hopefully you have better luck with that high priest than I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll work it out. I'll see you back in Dorgesh-Kaan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Clearly. So how can I get you out?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I had a moving-over-distance sphere with me, but they confiscated it when they put me in here. They didn't even know what it was. They were just frightened that it might explode.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll look around and see if I can find it.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Is that you, [player name]? Do you have that moving-over-distance sphere?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The guards confiscated it when they put me in here. It must be somewhere nearby.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it here.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]! I'm sorry to leave you, but it looks like you'll have to find out about Yu'biusk on your own. Hopefully you have better luck with that high priest than I did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll work it out. I'll see you back in Dorgesh-Kaan.

sections.title: Talking to Zanik in Oldak's lab

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You're back! Any luck at the temple? Have you found out where Yu'biusk is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Don't give up! I know you'll work it out.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. Have you found out anything about Yu'biusk yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: I'm sure you'll uncover something soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me want to know about Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That knowledge not for ordinary goblin. Only those who understand ways of Big High War God may learn of Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand Big High War God.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Then you answer my questions, and we will see.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: True or false: Those who do not believe in Big High War God, whether they goblins or other races, must die.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: True.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Oh? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Big High War God commands it.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That right! It in commandments.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: 'Not to doubt Big High War God! Doubters must die!' This mean we slay goblins who not believe.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: 'Always to slay enemies of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Second question. True or false: Big High War God chose goblins to be his race because goblins mighty warriors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: True.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Really? Then how we come to be mighty warriors, before Big High War God find us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We taught selves to be mighty!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Fool! Heretic! You guilty of sin of pride! Goblins teach selves nothing. All good we have come from Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Other god made us mighty.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You confused. Yes, in time of great war, some goblins serve other gods. But no one make us mighty but Big High War God.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Look around at how mighty we are! How we proud of war. How we never flinch from battle or pain or death. How we never surrender or show mercy or pity!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: How could any other god but Big High War God do this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We naturally mighty warriors.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Naturally? Let me tell you what goblins are naturally! We tiny, we weak, we cowardly, we love peace and not war!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: All that is good, that is strong, that is powerful, we get from Big High War God! Go away and remember this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: False.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Oh? Why you say that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He chose us for other quality.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: What other quality important, except that we great warriors? Get away, silly goblin! Too much drink muddle brain and give you strange thoughts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goblins not mighty warriors before he chose us.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That good! Goblins mighty warriors now, but that only because Big High War God make us so! Without Big High War God we nothing! Remember that.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Third question. True or false: Goblin leaders should be good at planning in order to win battles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: True.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Oh? Why you say that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Big High War God rewards victory.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Even victory that comes through clever dirty tricks? Even victory that comes through breaking Big High War God's commandments? No. Not all victory honour Big High War God.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Big High War God teaches us to think for selves.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: What? Where you hear that? You think for yourself, you maybe think stupid, dangerous thoughts. You maybe think war not good, or Big High War God not best god.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: No. Do not think for self. Think only thoughts of Big High War God. His thoughts perfect, so if you think different thoughts, those thoughts bad.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That why Big High War God command, 'Not to make own plans. Thinkers must die.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Planning is part of war, and all parts of war are good.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You confused. It true all parts of war are good, but not all parts of war are for goblins.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Big High War God not make us commanders. He make us soldiers, and send commanders to lead us. They make plans for us. We not have commander now, but still we must not make own plans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: False.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That one of the commandments.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That right. 'Not to make own plans. Thinkers must die.'

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Last question. Prophecy say someday Big High War God send Chosen Commander. What Chosen Commander do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lead goblins to promised land of Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Yu'biusk? You have story wrong way round! Yu'biusk land all goblins come from. But there no prophecy that we go back there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lead goblins to victory over whole world.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That right! And war will end in victory and victory last forever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Destroy world.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Destroy world? Why Big High War God want to do that? Then there be no goblins to fight for him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Make goblins fight each other.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Ha ha ha! Big High War God not need send commander to do that! Only reason goblins not fight more is prophet Hopespear say not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Big High War God controls outcome of the battle.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That true. But it true whether goblin leaders make plans or not. So it not answer question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goblins stupid and would make poor plans.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You call me stupid? Big High War God make goblins perfect! Big High War God tell us not make plans, but if we make plans, they be best plans ever!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goblins not mighty warriors.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: How dare you? You insult goblin race in holy temple! We mighty warriors because we worship Big High War God!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Goblins like you, who say we not strong, you are thing that make us weak! Never doubt, never question, never think! That way of Big High War God.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They might lead us astray.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That wrong! No true goblin be lead astray by those who not believe. If goblin led astray, that goblin deserve to die!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: I think you weak in faith, if you think you be led astray. You go now, think on this. Be stronger in faith.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: False.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Oh? Why not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Big High War God commands it.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Big High War God command we not kill enemies?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You not understand Big High War God at all! You like filthy stinking cave goblin! Go away and think on how stupid you are!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goblin should not fight goblin.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: It true that prophet Hopespear tell us goblin should not fight against goblin. But Big High War God, he command that those who not believe must die.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: To little mind, weak mind, this seem to contradict. But only if you not have faith. In Big High War God, there no contradiction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We try to convert them instead.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Convert them? You stupid goblin! You not even know name of god. He Big High WAR God! We kill enemies, not convert!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I not know.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That good. True goblin not want to understand. Fight and die for glory of Big High War God. Never ask question or have thought.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is not for me then.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That good. True goblin not want to understand. Fight and die for glory of Big High War God. Never ask question or have thought.

sections.title: Talking to High Priest Bighead again before answering questions

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me want to know about Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You ready to answer questions?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That good. True goblin not want to understand. Fight and die for glory of Big High War God. Never ask question or have thought.

sections.title: Talking to High Priest Bighead after answering questions

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You have passed test. Now I answer your questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me want to know about Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Yu'biusk is goblin legend. Very old legend, from before this temple built. From before Hopespear the prophet. Even before great war.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Legend say that goblins come from land of Yu'biusk. Big High War God find goblins in Yu'biusk and bring them to other lands to train them as warriors!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will goblins return to Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Why we want to return to Yu'biusk? In Yu'biusk we weak. Big High War God take us from Yu'biusk and make us strong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Me not know. Me think Snothead maybe know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find him?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: He old high priest in temple crypt behind me! He dead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Maybe me ask him next time me see him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? But you said he dead!

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Ha ha! You not know, but since you pass test, me tell you.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: When goblin high priest die, they buried in temple crypt in special magic way.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: This mean next high priest can ask them questions, even though they dead. I just say name of high priest over grave, and he appear and answer questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I talk to old high priests?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: No. Temple crypt is holy place. Only high priest allowed into crypt, and that me. No other goblin ever go in.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Crypt door locked by six keys, kept by six tribal priests. Priests only give keys to me. They never give them to normal goblin like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What Big High War God's real name?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You ask big question. Big High War God's true name not for normal goblin minds, but since you pass test, I tell you. His true name Bandos.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: That name that ogres call him, that orks call him. That name that goblin priests call him on holy days. But normal goblins, you must show respect. You only call him Big High War God.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why there only six tribes in temple?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Goblin tribes are thing of past. Long ago, all goblins divided into twelve tribes. Dorgeshuun tribe rebel, so Big High War God punish them. Then there eleven tribes.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: No other tribes disappear like Dorgeshuun, but over ages, tribes merge. Now most goblins, they not know what tribe they come from.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Sometimes, goblins who know what tribe they come from, they come here and make tribal enclaves. They colour armour different colours to show what tribe they in. But not all tribes here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How me get into tribal enclaves?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Goblins show tribe by dying armour special tribe colour. Yellow for Yurkolgokh, white for Saragorgak, purple for Horogothgar, orange for Narogoshuun, blue for Ekeleshuun and black for Huzamogaarb.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: You not wearing colour, you not entering the enclave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where they get dye?

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Some goblins say they go to witch-lady in Draynor for it. Me hope they beat up witch-lady and take dye! That way of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me have no questions.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Good. Goblins should not ask questions. Fight and die for glory of Big High War God. Never ask question or have thought.

sections.title: Talking to High Priest Bighead after asking him all questions

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Me want to take test again.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Good. Goblins must always remember faith to Big High War God.

lines.speaker: High Priest Bighead
lines.text: Then bring glory to Big High War God.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make dyes for me please?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What sort of dye would you like? Red, yellow or blue?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make black or white dye?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Black dye can be made by grinding up black mushrooms. That's dead easy. The mushrooms grow in some old caves and ruins.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: But white dye? There's no such thing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how can you colour clothes white?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Usually you can't, but with poorly-made clothes, it's possible to remove the dye. Even then, it's a very tricky process involving a special ingredient found in the gut of the Hemenster Whitefish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hemenster Whitefish?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Yes, found only at Hemenster, oddly enough. It's the place where they have those fishing contests.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: If you fancy catching some, you should know that very specific bait is needed. Only slimy eels will work. I hear you can catch those in the caves below Lumbridge Swamp.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: Anyway, if you do have something you want the dye removing from, just bring me a Hemenster Whitefish and five coins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: You're welcome!

sections.title: Opening the gate next to Morris

lines.speaker: Morris
lines.text: Competition pass please.

lines.speaker: Morris
lines.text: Move on through. Talk to Bonzo to enter the competition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to catch a Hemenster Whitefish.

lines.speaker: Morris
lines.text: Whitefish, eh? Okay, since you've won the competition before, I'll let you in.

sections.title: Talking to Aggie while holding goblin mail

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make dyes for me please?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What sort of dye would you like? Red, yellow or blue?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remove the dye from this goblin mail?

sections.title: Using goblin mail on Aggie with necessary items

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remove the dye from this goblin mail?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: That'll be five coins please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much money on me.

sections.title: Using goblin mail on Aggie without whitefish

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remove the dye from this goblin mail?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: You'll need to go and get a Hemenster Whitefish first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I was just about to do that.

sections.title: Using whitefish on Aggie without any goblin mail

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remove the dye from this goblin mail?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: What goblin mail?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, right. I'll go and get some.

sections.title: Using white goblin mail on Aggie

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remove the dye from this goblin mail?

lines.speaker: Aggie
lines.text: That goblin mail is already white.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, so it is.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Listen! Whoever does not worship Big High War God, whether great or small, goblin or other, must die!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: If another goblin tell you to worship different gods, you not listen to them! You kill them! All tribe kill them!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Kill peoples who worship different gods! Smite them! Destroy them! Do not show mercy!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Enemies must die! Doubters must die! These words of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Remember, we holy people of Big High War God! He chose us to be special people, better than all people in world!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Big High War God not choose us because we many, or because we strong. We nothing before he choose us! But he take us from Yu'biusk with mighty hand, and make us strong!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Soon Big High War God send Chosen Commander!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: I tell you truth! There some goblins here who not die before they see Chosen Commander coming with glory of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Then Chosen Commander lead goblins into war as one army, and we kill every other race and all followers of other gods!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Then Big High War God himself return and sit on throne of bronze and rull over all!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Let me tell you story about Hopespear! He prophet of Big High War God! He very holy goblin!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: When Hopespear old, he walking near Goblin Village, and little goblin children came out of village and make fun of him.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: The he look at them, and curse them in name of Big High War God. Then monsters come and tear children to pieces!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: May Big High War God give you faith!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Before you enter battle, clear mind of thought. Do not think about what you will do or what may happen.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: When battle start, Big High War God guide your arm and fill your heart with courage.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Have faith in Big High War God. He rule all war, so whatever happen in war, it his will.

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Goblins great nation in time of great war!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Big High War God himself lead us into battle! Then we crush enemies, we burn cities to ground, we kill every man, woman and child! Even animals!

lines.speaker: Priest
lines.text: Some enemies, we kill them so bad, now there no trace and no one ever remember them! That what happen to enemies of Big High War God!

lines.speaker: Snothead
lines.text: You beat me. What you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: Snothead
lines.text: It long way away. Me not know where. High priest who come before me, he say that when goblins die we go to Yu'biusk. Perhaps he know where it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was your predecessor's name?

lines.speaker: Snothead
lines.text: Snailfeet. That his name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you attack me?

lines.speaker: Snothead
lines.text: You not goblin. Not high priest. So me attack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like being dead?

lines.speaker: Snothead
lines.text: It dark. Very dark. And cold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Snailfeet
lines.text: You beaten me. Ask your questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: Snailfeet
lines.text: Far, far away. Me not know where. But high priest who come before me, he say that when goblins die in battle, our souls travel to Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was your predecessor's name?

lines.speaker: Snailfeet
lines.text: Mosschin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you attack me?

lines.speaker: Snailfeet
lines.text: You not goblin high priest! Me only answer questions for goblin high priest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like being dead?

lines.speaker: Snailfeet
lines.text: Why me here? Me die in battle, fighting humans! Why me not in Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Mosschin
lines.text: You have defeated me, human. Ask your questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: Mosschin
lines.text: Far beyond this world. Long ago, the Big High War God brought goblins from Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: Mosschin
lines.text: The high priest who came before me said that whenever goblins die in battle and their blood spills onto the ground, Yu'biusk is close and their souls can travel there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was your predecessor's name?

lines.speaker: Mosschin
lines.text: Redeyes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you attack me?

lines.speaker: Mosschin
lines.text: I only answer questions for those who are worthy! I had to test you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like being dead?

lines.speaker: Mosschin
lines.text: All alone. I thought my soul would travel to Yu'biusk. But perhaps I was not worthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Mosschin
lines.text: Goodbye, human.

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: You have defeated me, human. Ask your questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: Yu'biusk is far away. Beyond the icelands to the north, beyond the mountains to the west, beyond the oceans to the south and the deserts to the east.

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: But it is also close. Wherever goblins fight in the name of the Big High War God and their blood spills on the ground, Yu'biusk is close.

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: My predecessor told me that no living goblin has ever gone to Yu'biusk. If no living goblin can go there, then perhaps the dead can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was your predecessor's name?

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: Strongbones. He was a mighty high priest and warrior of the Big High War God. It was he who learned the magic that allows us to be called up to speak with the living.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you attack me?

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: You are not a goblin high priest, so I had to see if you were worthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like being dead?

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: I had thought perhaps I would go to Yu'biusk, to fight in endless battles for the Big High War God. Instead, there is only endless sleep.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Redeyes
lines.text: Good luck in your quest, human.

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: You have proved yourself the stronger, human. Ask your questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is Yu'biusk?

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: Yu'biusk is another plane of existence. No goblin alive today has ever been there.

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: It is many millennia since the Big High War God brought our ancestors from Yu'biusk, but the call to that plane is still strong in us.

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: If the barriers between the planes were ever to be broken, goblin blood would call out and open a portal to Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was your predecessor's name?

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: His name is not important. He is not buried here. Before my time, priests were cremated, and their ashes scattered to the winds. You cannot talk to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you attack me?

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: You are a human, not one of the children of the Big High War God. For this reason, you had to prove yourself in combat before I could speak with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like being dead?

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: I cannot tell you. I no longer remember what it is like to be alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Strongbones
lines.text: Goodbye, human. I am sure that the Chosen Commander will find what she is looking for when you bring her to Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... What?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: You're back! Any luck at the temple? Have you found out where Yu'biusk is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sort of. I've discovered that Yu'biusk is another plane of existence. Apparently if the barriers between the planes are breached, goblin blood will call out to Yu'biusk and create a portal leading there.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Another plane? You mean goblins aren't native to Gielinor? That's remarkable. Think of the historical implications...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how could we breach the barriers between the planes?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I know! The fungus ring in the southern caves! Didn't you say that had magical properties, Oldak?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Yes... that sample you showed me had very similar properties to Law runes. I believe that the fungus ring could create an effect similar to my moving-over-distance spheres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fungus ring? That sounds like a fairy ring. They're part of a big teleportation network.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Well that confirms it then. This fungus ring might be just what we need!

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: If the ring can create a moving-over-distance effect... then with enough power, we should be able to use it to break the planar barriers.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Then we just need goblin blood to call out to Yu'biusk!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Well I reckon we've got plenty of that.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I can't believe this! The goblin homeland! This could be somewhere safe for goblins like Grubfoot. A new home, free of war!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Could this be it, [player name]? Could this be my destiny? Could this be why I was marked by the Tears of Guthix?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: We should see if this works before we tell anyone else. Now, I think I can create some apparatus to provide power to the fungus ring.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Come on, Zanik. [player name], will you meet us by the fungus ring to the south of the city?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Zanik. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Hey, [player name]. This is incredible! I can't believe we're really going to go to Yu'biusk, the Land of the Goblins!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think we'll find there?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I don't know. All we have to go on is Grubfoot's dream, which isn't much. Still, not long now until we find out!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Anyway, you should help Oldak out with the machine. He wants me to stand here so that when the portal opens, it'll have a goblin right next to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. The machine is nearly ready. When I activate it, it will bombard the fungus ring with magical energy.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: For this to work, we'll need to make sure the power is distributed correctly between the three circles of fungi. I need to handle the activation. [player name], could you please handle the power distribution?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: The inner circle has a radius of one zurg, the middle circle has a radius of two zurgs, and the outer circle has a radius of three zurgs. You will need to distribute the power in proportion to the area of each circle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Oldak! It's working!

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Something must have gone wrong. This isn't what Grubfoot described.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: No, no, no...

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: I don't understand. What happened here?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: We must be missing something. This can't be it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe we should look around.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Yes, let's.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: I'll stay here and keep an eye on the portal.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: You should have a look around and see if you can find out anything about this place. I'll stay here and keep an eye on the portal.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: [player name]. We should head back through the portal. Zanik is waiting on the other side.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Don't go too far! I don't know how stable this portal is!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: There must be something around here. It can't all be dead... can it?

sections.title: Talking to Grubfoot after visiting Yu'Biusk but before opening the strange box

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Did you go to Land of Goblins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but we haven't properly explored it yet.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Shut up Grubfoot!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Shut up Grubfoot!

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: This is really it, isn't it? Grubfoot's dream was just that, a dream. All Yu'biusk is now is an empty wasteland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand... What happened here?

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: The Big High War God happened.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: I thought this might be it, [player name]. I really did. I thought this place might finally give me some answers, but it's just another dead end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, Zanik.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: Let's have a look at what this box is, then let's go home. There's nothing here for anyone any more.

lines.speaker: Zanik
lines.text: What's happening? [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik!

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: The portal is closing! You've got to get back now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Zanik...

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: [player name]? What's going on? Where's Zanik?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's gone.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Gone? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know... She just disappeared...

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: No... Zanik...

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: The portal... We have to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll find you Zanik.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Hello, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are we going to do about Zanik?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: We can only assume she's still in Yu'biusk somewhere. Problem is, I haven't been able to get the fungus ring machine working again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So there's no way back?

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Perhaps not. Still, now that we've broken the planar barriers between Gielinor and Yu'biusk, maybe we've enabled some alternate methods of transport there.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Those fungus rings would be a good starting point. Maybe you could use one of them to find another way to Yu'biusk. In the meantime, I'll keep trying to get the machine working again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to get back to Yu'biusk.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: You did? Did you find anything there? Was there any trace of Zanik?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, there was nothing.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: Then it's just as I feared. It seems that whatever's happened to Zanik, wherever she is... she's on her own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will find her, Oldak.

lines.speaker: Oldak
lines.text: I know you will, [player name].

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Did you go to Land of Goblins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but it wasn't how you'd dreamed. It was a devastated wasteland.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Yu'biusk... Land of Goblins... All gone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid so. And that's not all. Zanik vanished there.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: Chosen Commander? She vanish?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. A bright white light picked her up and then she was gone.

lines.speaker: Grubfoot
lines.text: She not gone. I sure of it. Someday she return.

lines.speaker: General Bentnoze
lines.text: Shut up Grubfoot!

lines.speaker: General Wartface
lines.text: Shut up Grubfoot!
